page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, yielding an 8 GB per vCPU ratio. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it utilizes an Intel processor with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data places the n4-highmem-8 in the average performance tier for most compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1815.0 and a multi-core score of 6984.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 13039.39. Text processing is a relative strength, scoring 2350.0 in Geekbench, which falls into the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is measured at 542.3 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 61.99 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For database operations, it handles 9.89 million Redis SET operations per second. However, text generation for medium-sized 7B language models is a performance bottleneck, registering a weak score of 6.1 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, the n4-highmem-8 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-performance local storage or GPU acceleration. The inclusion of Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions provides hardware-level acceleration for vector and matrix operations without requiring dedicated coprocessors. This server is configured for workloads such as in-memory databases, text processing pipelines, caching layers like Redis, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute capacity.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 8 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, providing an 8 GB per vCPU ratio. The underlying Intel processor features 4 physical cores, a 260 MB L3 cache, and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average performance across most compute and memory metrics, with a Passmark CPU score of 13039.39 and a Redis throughput of 9.89 million SET operations per second. Text processing is a notable strength, placing in the top 25% of benchmarks, whereas medium-scale LLM text generation represents a performance bottleneck. The server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing applications that benefit from a high memory-to-core ratio without requiring local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 8 Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and AMX support, delivering average compute performance and strong text processing capabilities.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 8 Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and advanced AMX and AVX-512 instruction support.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and hardware-accelerated matrix instructions for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 64 GB RAM providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for hardware-accelerated vector math
Strong text processing performance, scoring in the top 25% of benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the default configuration
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing pipelines
